Fix pivoting-agent lint: put search_skills/list_skills on same line as "do not"

was split across two lines, so the line with the function names didn't match.

commit.message:
V1 fallback: fix skip logic — check date coverage not just priced rows

Rooms with a few V2 rows (all priced) were skipped because all_priced
check only compared existing rows. Villa Loupa had 5 priced rows but
rate rules cover 210 dates. Now skip only when existing priced rows
outnumber rate rule dates (PriceLabs rooms with full coverage).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

V1 fallback: INSERT rows for all dates covered by rate rules

Previously the fallback had separate UPDATE (for existing rows) and
INSERT (for zero-row rooms) paths. Rooms like Villa Loupa had a few V2
rows with prices, so they hit the UPDATE path which only touched NULL
prices — missing dates were never created. Now uses a single unified
path: INSERT ON CONFLICT for every date in rate rules, preserving
existing V2/PriceLabs prices with COALESCE.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: rewrite CLAUDE.md for inertia-manipulation project

Replace OSX-UR specific content with project-specific instructions
from _CLAUDE.md, incorporating reusable coding standards (ruff,
conventional commits, docstring style).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove osx_bilateral relative path references

These path context notes were remnants from the previous project
and are not applicable to the current inertia-manipulation project.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Global fix: write price column in all room_availability sync paths

Every adapter (Beds24, Calry, Smoobu, Hostfully, Hostaway, Elevate)
was writing cm_price but not the price column. Guest-facing code reads
price, so all availability rows had NULL prices for guests. Fixed all
~40 INSERT/UPDATE statements across all sync paths (manual, cron,
connection-level, admin full pricing, linked rooms, V1 fallback).

Also backfilled 1,613 rows for account 220.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: セキュリティ改善の実践記録を成長日記に追記

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: modularize JS into ES modules under docs/scripts/

Split 2400+ line index.html monolith into 10 focused modules:

  scripts/page-ui.js        — navbar, hero particles, typewriter, reveals
  scripts/fx/state.js       — shared mutable state & constants
  scripts/fx/canvas.js      — shared 2D canvas context
  scripts/fx/audio.js       — Web Audio engine (SFX + 8-bit BGM)
  scripts/fx/particles.js   — shotgun blast & muzzle flash pool
  scripts/fx/shake.js       — element-level tilt shake
  scripts/fx/monsters.js    — pixel-art sprites, pool, projectile firing
  scripts/fx/cursor.js      — custom crosshair with ammo & cooldown ring
  scripts/fx/game.js        — HP, damage, game-over modal, leaderboard
  scripts/fx/main.js        — orchestrates all modules + game loop

Additional fixes:
- Fire cooldown (0.22s) prevents rapid-fire spam; visualized as orange
  sweep arc on the crosshair ring
- Touch: only touchstart fires (touchmove no longer triggers shots)
- index.html reduced from 2414 → 1406 lines

commit.message:
feat: progressive Seeknal tutorial with pipeline fixes (#1)

* feat: add 8-module progressive Seeknal tutorial

Create a complete, progressive tutorial for undergraduate students
learning real-world data pipelines using Seeknal. The tutorial covers
the full pipeline lifecycle from CSV ingestion to production patterns.

Tutorial modules:
- Module 1: Introduction & Setup (sources, REPL)
- Module 2: Data Transformation (SQL, ref(), DAG, safety workflow)
- Module 3: Data Quality & Testing (rules, profiling)
- Module 4: Advanced Sources & CDC (JSONL, Parquet, merge)
- Module 5: DRY Config & Python API (@source, @transform)
- Module 6: Feature Engineering for ML (@feature_group, RFM, SOA)
- Module 7: Semantic Layer & Metrics (measures, exposures)
- Module 8: Production Patterns (environments, lineage)

Reference guides: QUICKSTART.md, QUERY_GUIDE.md

Codebase fixes:
- Add unique_customers measure to semantic model
- Fix hardcoded date in customer_daily_agg.py
- Add columns to sales_snapshot.yml
- Create order validation rules for Module 3
- Update README to match 8-module structure

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: change order quality rules to warn severity so pipeline runs without errors

The order_not_null and order_revenue_valid rules had severity: error which
halted the pipeline when detecting expected quality issues in raw data.
Changed to severity: warn so the full tutorial pipeline completes end-to-end.
Updated Module 3 tutorial text to reflect warning behavior and added a tip
about using error severity in production.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

feat: セッション作成画面（フロントエンド）を実装

3ステップのウィザード形式でセッションを作成できる機能を実装。
シナリオとアバターを選択してセッションを開始できます。

## 実装内容

### APIクライアント
- **scenarios.ts** - シナリオAPI統合
  - listScenarios: 一覧取得（ページネーション、フィルター対応）
  - createScenario: 新規作成
  - getScenario: 詳細取得

- **avatars.ts** - アバターAPI統合
  - listAvatars: 一覧取得（タイプ・スタイルフィルター対応）
  - createAvatar: 新規作成
  - getAvatar: 詳細取得

### UI実装
- **/dashboard/sessions/new** - セッション作成ページ
  - Step 1: シナリオ選択（カード形式、検索機能）
  - Step 2: アバター選択（サムネイル付き、フィルター機能）
  - Step 3: オプション設定（メタデータ入力）
  - プログレスインジケーター
  - バリデーション付きフォーム

- **/dashboard/sessions/[id]** - セッション詳細ページ
  - ステータス表示
  - シナリオ・アバター情報
  - メタデータ表示
  - セッション操作（TODO: プレイヤー実装予定）

### 多言語対応
- **en/sessions.json** - 英語翻訳
- **ja/sessions.json** - 日本語翻訳
  - セッション作成画面の全UI文言
  - バリデーションメッセージ
  - エラーメッセージ

### 機能
- ✅ シナリオ選択（検索、カテゴリフィルター）
- ✅ アバター選択（サムネイル、タイプ・スタイルフィルター）
- ✅ セッション作成（メタデータ対応）
- ✅ セッション詳細表示
- ✅ エラーハンドリング
- ✅ ローディング状態管理

## 関連タスク
- Task #22: セッション作成画面実装 ✅ 完了

## 次のステップ
- セッションプレイヤー（リアルタイム会話UI）実装
- 録画・録音機能実装
- WebSocket統合（リアルタイム通信）

commit.message:
Switch Make/Test stages from gpt-oss:20b to qwen3.5:9b model

## Model Change
- Replaced gpt-oss:20b (13GB, 20B params) → qwen3.5:9b (6.6GB, 9B params)
- qwen3.5:9b optimized for creative writing & ad generation
- Smaller model footprint improves inference latency
- Quality gain for copywriting tasks outweighs speed reduction

## Port Update
- Remote Ollama instance moved to port 11435 (was 11434)
- Updated defaults in ollama.ts + SettingsModal.tsx
- Still respects VITE_OLLAMA_HOST env var for flexibility
- Backward compatible: users can override in Settings

## Benchmark Result
- gpt-oss:20b: 16.3s @ 26 tok/s for 323 tokens (ad headlines)
- qwen3.5:9b: Better quality output for creative tasks (cold-start loading)
- Decision: quality > speed for Make stage (copy generation)

## Build Status
- Zero TypeScript errors
- 961.88 kB JS bundle
- All components compile successfully

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add CrewAI multi-agent system + Jetson gateway stack

- crewai-server/: FastAPI :8000 wrapping a 4-agent sequential crew
  - Planner (Gemini 3.1 Pro) → Reviewer (GPT-5.3) → Coder (Claude Opus 4.6) → Executor (Haiku + paramiko SSH)
  - /crew/kickoff, /status, /jobs, /cancel, /health endpoints
  - Thread-safe job store with JSON persistence
  - HPC SSH tool via paramiko for remote experiment execution
- deployments/docker-compose.yml: ttyd ARM64 container for Jetson (:7681)
- scripts/: idempotent install_gateway, setup_ssh_trust, setup_macbook
- tests/: bash -n syntax checks + docker compose validation
- docs/: SECURITY_POLICY, updated DEVELOPMENT_GUIDE with Jetson topology
- README: Mermaid architecture diagram + 3-step quickstart
- .gitignore: added *.key, *.pem, crewai runtime exclusions

commit.message:
セキュリティ設定追加

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove falsified Paper #8 claims, promote Prediction Letter as primary reference

Paper #8's central theorem (c_2 ~ L^{2/9}) was falsified by ultra-fine TM data
showing c_2 ~ ln(L). The "0.054% deviation" from Richardson extrapolation was a
pre-asymptotic artifact. The d_R = 10/9 exponent remains valid as an empirical
observable from |R| scaling (Prediction Letter), but Paper #8's analytical
derivation route is incorrect. Updated all references accordingly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix webhook toasts: pass project/service names via SSE, skip removed events

The SSE payload was only sending {id, status} which meant the client
couldn't resolve project/service names for new deployments (their IDs
don't exist in local state yet). Now the SSE includes projectName,
serviceName, and event type from the webhook payload directly.

Also fixes Deployment.removed events incorrectly showing status SUCCESS
(the deployment's previous status) instead of REMOVED, and skips removed
events on the client since they're just cleanup noise.

Adds comprehensive CLAUDE.md with full project documentation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: fix concurrency group to prevent container name collisions

Use github.ref instead of github.sha so multiple pushes to main
cancel stale runs instead of running in parallel (which causes
Docker container name conflicts on shared runners).

commit.message:
fix: init order — run migration before create_tables for v5 databases (#104)

create_tables defines v6 schema (24-column entries + indexes on topic,
category, etc). On existing v5 databases, the entries table has (id, data)
— no topic column. CREATE INDEX fails before migration can decompose blobs.

Fix: ensure counters table exists first, run migrate_if_needed, then
create_tables. Also guard migration against fresh databases (no entries
table yet) to avoid migrating empty DBs.

commit.message:
Fix infinite loading screen after auth refactor

getSession fires reliably on page load and is the correct way to
initialize auth state. onAuthStateChange INITIAL_SESSION is async
and can be delayed, causing loading to stay true indefinitely.

Pattern: getSession handles init + setLoading(false); onAuthStateChange
handles subsequent events (sign-in, sign-out, token refresh) with
INITIAL_SESSION explicitly skipped to avoid double fetchChildProfile.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Show ~Xs/Seite hint for multi-page PDFs in playlist (same as galleries)

PDFs and galleries use identical duration-splitting logic in buildSlides(),
so the admin hint is equally useful for both. Page count is looked up from
pdf_renders per display, shown only when page_count > 1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: diagnostic logging for index_folder — skip counts and no-symbol tracking

* feat: add per-reason discovery logging and diagnostic response fields to index_folder

Adds structured skip_counts tracking to discover_local_files() covering all
filter paths (symlink, path_traversal, skip_pattern, gitignore, extra_ignore,
secret, wrong_extension, too_large, unreadable, binary). Files that pass
discovery but yield no AST symbols are now tracked separately with DEBUG
logging. Both datasets are returned in the index_folder tool response as
discovery_skip_counts, no_symbols_count, and no_symbols_files, making it
straightforward to diagnose why files are absent from the index.

Adds --log-level / --log-file CLI args to server main() (also via
JCODEMUNCH_LOG_LEVEL / JCODEMUNCH_LOG_FILE env vars). Defaults to WARNING
so normal operation is silent. Updates README and MCP tool description
to document the new flags and response fields. Fixes test_security.py
call sites to unpack the new 3-tuple return value.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: trim index_folder tool description to reduce context bloat

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: surface diagnostic fields in incremental index result

Add discovery_skip_counts, no_symbols_count, and no_symbols_files to
the incremental return path in index_folder, which previously omitted
them despite being computed. Also fix test unpacking __, __ -> *_.
